project.name: mccoy-tyner
project.namespace_status: DECIDED 2026-06-10: _jazzcanon. Schema created at Phase 4 (CREATE SCHEMA). Product name still deferred; mccoy-tyner remains the working codename.
project.platform: Standalone Postgres 16 on vps8-core (127.0.0.1:5433), shared instance, project-owned schema
project.status: Phase 3 design complete. Applied at Phase 4. No Phase 1-2 data exists yet (contract-first).

domainModel.summary: Curated canon of ~100 post-bop/pre-fusion jazz albums with track-level personnel, source-grounded with epistemic labels, for a searchable discovery app.
core_decisions: People (performers, leaders, producers, engineers, composers) are ONE 'person' entity.
Every fact-bearing row carries epistemic (obs/inf/unk) + citation provenance.
Identity-resolution pass (name -> person uuid) is required Phase 4 ingest work; not in research data.

tables.name: source
tables.purpose: Global source registry (replaces per-file S1/S2 maps).

tables.name: album
tables.purpose: One row per album; PK is the join key to all research data.

tables.name: track
tables.purpose: One row per track per album.

tables.name: track_composer
tables.purpose: Composers as persons; enables 'compositions by X'.

tables.name: citation
tables.purpose: Provenance with FK integrity; one fact per row.

tables.name: album_art
tables.purpose: Cover images: FILES on disk (data/album-art/), path+metadata+provenance in the row, never bytes. Phase 2 captures refs; Phase 4 fetches/stores.

relationships: album 1:N session, track, performance, production_credit (all ON DELETE CASCADE)
person 1:N performance, production_credit, track_composer, person_name_variant; person 1:0..1 album.leader
performance M:N track (performance_track); performance M:N session (performance_session)
album M:N style (album_style); album N:1 label, style, studio(via session)
functions: fn_degrees_between(person_a uuid, person_b uuid) -> int  (six-degrees, recursive CTE over co-occurrence)

semantic_search.engine_1_pgvector: album.embedding + person.embedding (vector(768)), HNSW cosine. Built from composed search_document text (see v_album_search_source / v_person_search_source).
semantic_search.engine_2_relational: Networks via SQL: six-degrees (fn_degrees_between), sideman co-occurrence (v_sideman_network), timelines (v_musician_timeline).
semantic_search.embedding_model: DECIDED 2026-06-10: local nomic-embed-text (768-dim) via Ollama on vps4 (free, self-hosted, working-first). Reversible — re-embedding the ~500-doc corpus is <1 min / pennies.
semantic_search.static_export: Corpus small enough to precompute album x album neighbors and export static JSON; static site gets full similarity discovery with no live backend. Only free-text NL search needs a backend (Phase 4 decision).
semantic_search.foundry: Decision deferred: keep embeddings local to _jazzcanon vs also push to _foundry.embeddings/_foundry.documents for cross-project RAG. Recommend local, door open.

agentReadiness.explainability: epistemic labels + citation rows exposed in detail views answer 'why is this here / how sure are we'.

security.model: Single-user, bearer-token at app middleware (per intent-defaults). RLS optional defense-in-depth, not required for v1.
security.note: Roles namespaced to survive DROP SCHEMA, per database-conventions.md. Passwords from vault (projectname_* secret).

steps: resolve person identities (name -> uuid, surface borderline merges to John)
resolve MBID + fetch album art (Cover Art Archive -> iTunes fallback) into data/album-art/ + album_art rows
ingest.idempotency: keyed on album.id and resolved person.id; JSON files remain git-tracked human-editable source of truth

rationale.decision: Project-owned schema in shared Postgres, not a standalone DB
rationale.why: Shared infra (pgvector, pg_cron, backups) + hard isolation via DROP SCHEMA CASCADE. Mirrors the _foundry pattern; standalone DB only justified for apps owning their own migrations (Honcho).
Performers, producers, engineers, composers are all people; required for engineer->sessions->albums and composer-works queries, and consolidates identity resolution.
Source-grounded discovery is the product's differentiator; the app renders confidence. FK-backed citation beats text[] of source ids for integrity.

rationale.decision: Identity-resolution pass flagged as real Phase 4 work
rationale.why: Research data has no person ids, only name strings + variants; 'Bill Evans'/'William John Evans' must collapse to one person.

pgvector can't do graph traversal; recursion can't do fuzzy similarity. Tiny corpus lets a build step precompute neighbors and ship a static site, honoring the simplicity intent while maximizing discovery.

rationale.decision: Album art split: Phase 2 finds refs, Phase 4 grabs/stores files; files on disk not in DB
rationale.why: Identification is research (agents); binary fetch is deterministic plumbing (script). Cover Art Archive (canonical, redistributable) primary, iTunes fallback. Files under data/album-art/ keep the DB lean and feed the static bundle directly. MBID also stored as a durable external key for future enrichment.
Cheap provenance of the Orchestrator ballot; renders a 'why it's in the canon' story.

rationale.decision: Namespace decided as _jazzcanon (2026-06-10); mccoy-tyner stays the codename
rationale.why: A codename is free; the durable name only costs when bound to schema/roles/URL at Phase 4. John chose _jazzcanon now since nothing was bound through Phase 3 — zero cost. Product name still open.

openDecisions: Embeddings local vs pushed to _foundry (recommend local).
Live NL-search backend vs static-only (recommend defer/static-first).
Product (public-facing) name — distinct from the _jazzcanon namespace.
